title: Bench Press: Barbell
primer: The barbell bench press is a compound exercise that primarily targets the chest muscles.
steps: Lie flat on the bench with feet firmly on the floor
Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder width
Unrack the bar and lower it to mid-chest
Press the bar back up to starting position
tips: Keep your shoulder blades retracted throughout the movement
Maintain a slight arch in your lower back
Don't bounce the bar off your chest

title: Squat: Barbell
primer: The barbell squat is a fundamental compound exercise for lower body strength.
steps: Position the bar on your upper back
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart
Lower down by bending at hips and knees
Push through heels to return to standing
tips: Keep your chest up and core engaged
Aim for at least parallel depth

title: Deadlift: Barbell
primer: The deadlift is one of the most effective exercises for overall strength development.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart, bar over mid-foot
Bend down and grip the bar just outside your legs
Keep your back straight and lift the bar by extending hips and knees
Lower the bar back to the ground with control
tips: Keep the bar close to your body throughout

title: Pull-up
primer: Pull-ups are an excellent bodyweight exercise for back and arm development.
steps: Hang from a bar with hands slightly wider than shoulders
Pull yourself up until chin is over the bar
Lower yourself back down with control
tips: Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together
Full range of motion is key

title: Overhead Press: Barbell
primer: The overhead press builds shoulder strength and stability.
steps: Start with bar at shoulder height
Press the bar straight overhead
Lock out arms at the top
Lower back to shoulders with control

title: Bicep Curl: Dumbbell
primer: Dumbbell curls isolate and build the bicep muscles.
steps: Stand with dumbbells at your sides, palms forward
Curl the weights up while keeping elbows stationary
Squeeze at the top
Lower with control
tips: Keep elbows close to your body

title: Tricep Extension: Dumbbell
primer: Tricep extensions target the back of the upper arm.
steps: Hold dumbbell overhead with both hands
Lower the weight behind your head
Extend arms back to starting position

title: Leg Press: Machine
primer: The leg press is a compound exercise for leg development using a machine.
steps: Sit in the machine with feet on the platform
Lower the weight by bending knees
Press through heels to extend legs
tips: Keep lower back against the pad

title: Lat Pulldown: Machine
primer: Lat pulldowns target the back muscles, similar to pull-ups.
steps: Sit at the machine with knees secured
Grip the bar wider than shoulders
Pull the bar down to upper chest
Return with control

title: Romanian Deadlift: Barbell
primer: The Romanian deadlift emphasizes the hamstrings and glutes.
steps: Hold bar at hip level with slight knee bend
Hinge at hips, lowering bar down legs
Keep back straight throughout
Return to standing by driving hips forward
tips: Bar should stay close to legs

title: Plank
primer: The plank is a core stability exercise.
steps: Start in push-up position on forearms
Keep body in straight line from head to heels
Hold position while engaging core

title: Crunch
steps: Lie on back with knees bent
Place hands behind head
Lift shoulders off ground by contracting abs
Lower back down with control

title: Lunge: Dumbbell
primer: Lunges are a unilateral leg exercise that builds strength and balance.
steps: Hold dumbbells at sides
Step forward with one leg
Lower back knee toward ground
Push through front heel to return
tips: Front knee should not pass toes

title: Lateral Raise: Dumbbell
primer: Lateral raises target the side deltoid muscles.
steps: Stand with dumbbells at sides
Raise arms out to sides to shoulder height
Lower with control

title: Row: Barbell
primer: Barbell rows build back thickness and strength.
steps: Bend at hips holding barbell
Pull bar to lower chest
Squeeze shoulder blades together
Lower with control
tips: Pull with your back, not arms
